summary: Wired QuickBooks token auto-refresh into sync/export routes. Added `get_authenticated_qb_client()` helper that fetches the QB connection, checks if the access token is expired/near-expiry (5-min buffer), calls `refresh_token()` if needed, persists new tokens to DB, and returns a ready-to-use `QuickBooksClient`. Also extracted `build_qb_oauth()` and `qb_environment()` helpers to reduce duplication. Replaced the duplicated connection-fetch + hard-error-on-expiry + client-build blocks in all three routes (`sync_vendors`, `sync_accounts`, `export_invoice_to_quickbooks`) with single calls to the helper. Added two tests: `test_token_storage_has_refresh_token` (verifies QBTokenStorage has refresh_token field) and `test_quickbooks_environment_token_url` (verifies refresh endpoint URL is correct).
decisions_made: Used 5-minute buffer before token expiry to proactively refresh rather than waiting until exact expiry
Placed helper functions at end of file to minimize diff and avoid moving existing route handlers
Helper fetches its own DB pool since AppState.db.tenant() is cheap and routes still need their own pool for subsequent queries
next_steps: Manual verification: connect QB, wait >1 hour, hit sync/vendors - should auto-refresh instead of erroring
Consider extracting the OAuth builder pattern further in the connect/disconnect/callback handlers (not in scope for this ticket)
